question: What is an appropriate general A1C target for most non-pregnant adults with type 2 diabetes?
gold_answer: For many non-pregnant adults, a general A1C goal is <7% when it can be achieved safely; individualization is recommended based on comorbidity and hypoglycemia risk.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Glycemic Targets (Section 6), executive summary; NICE NG28 glycaemic management overview.

question: How often should an A1C test be performed in a stable patient meeting treatment goals?
gold_answer: At least twice per year for patients meeting goals and with stable glycemic control; quarterly if therapy has changed or goals are not met.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Glycemic Assessment; RACGP Diabetes Handbook, monitoring schedule.

question: What annual screening is recommended for diabetic kidney disease in adults with type 2 diabetes?
gold_answer: Annual assessment of urinary albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) and estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR).
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Chronic Kidney Disease & Risk Management; KDIGO diabetes-CKD guidance.

question: How frequently should adults with diabetes receive a dilated retinal eye examination?
gold_answer: At diagnosis for type 2 diabetes, then typically every 1–2 years if no retinopathy and glycemia is stable; more often if retinopathy is present.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Retinopathy; NHMRC diabetic retinopathy guidelines.

question: What is the recommended blood pressure target for most adults with diabetes?
gold_answer: A common target is <140/90 mmHg for many, with a lower target such as <130/80 mmHg considered if it can be achieved safely based on cardiovascular risk and tolerability.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Hypertension/BP Management; ACC/AHA guidance contextualized for diabetes.

question: Who should receive statin therapy among adults with diabetes?
gold_answer: Most adults aged 40–75 with diabetes should receive at least moderate-intensity statin therapy, with high-intensity considered for additional ASCVD risk factors.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Lipid Management; ACC/AHA lipid guidelines.

question: What vaccinations are recommended for adults with diabetes?
gold_answer: Influenza annually; COVID-19 per public health guidance; age-appropriate pneumococcal vaccination; hepatitis B for unvaccinated adults up to 59 (and 60+ based on risk); and other routine adult vaccines per schedule.
gold_source_ref: CDC/ATAGI adult immunization schedules; ADA preventive care section.

question: How often should a comprehensive foot exam be performed for adults with diabetes?
gold_answer: At least annually, and more frequently if risk factors (neuropathy, deformity, prior ulcer) are present.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Neuropathy & Foot Care; IWGDF recommendations.

question: What lifestyle recommendations are supported for adults with type 2 diabetes regarding physical activity?
gold_answer: Aim for at least 150 minutes per week of moderate-to-vigorous aerobic activity spread over ≥3 days with no more than 2 consecutive days off, plus 2–3 sessions of resistance training and regular daily movement to reduce sedentary time.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Physical Activity; WHO physical activity guidelines.

question: Are Mediterranean-style or low-carbohydrate dietary patterns acceptable for adults with type 2 diabetes?
gold_answer: Yes—multiple evidence-based eating patterns such as Mediterranean-style, low-carbohydrate, or healthy plant-based can improve glycemia and cardiometabolic risk when individualized and nutritionally adequate.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Nutrition Therapy; Diabetes Australia evidence summaries.

question: When is metformin generally considered appropriate as initial pharmacologic therapy for type 2 diabetes?
gold_answer: Metformin is commonly used as initial pharmacologic therapy for most adults at diagnosis alongside lifestyle measures, unless contraindicated or not tolerated.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Pharmacologic Approaches; NICE NG28 initial therapy.

question: When should an SGLT2 inhibitor be considered for a person with type 2 diabetes and chronic kidney disease?
gold_answer: For adults with type 2 diabetes and CKD, an SGLT2 inhibitor is recommended to improve kidney and cardiovascular outcomes when eGFR and albuminuria criteria are met and the drug is not contraindicated.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, CKD & Risk Management; KDIGO Diabetes in CKD.

question: When should a GLP-1 receptor agonist be considered for a person with type 2 diabetes and established ASCVD or high CV risk?
gold_answer: A GLP-1 receptor agonist with cardiovascular benefit can be considered to reduce major adverse cardiovascular events in adults with T2D and established ASCVD or high risk.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Cardiovascular Disease & Risk Management.

question: What education should be provided to reduce the risk of hypoglycemia?
gold_answer: Educate on recognizing symptoms, checking glucose, treating promptly with fast-acting carbohydrate, reviewing precipitating factors, and adjusting activities in consultation with a clinician.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Hypoglycemia section; Diabetes self-management education standards.

question: Is continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) beneficial for adults with type 2 diabetes using insulin?
gold_answer: Yes—CGM can improve glycemic outcomes and reduce hypoglycemia in adults with T2D using insulin, when used consistently and with appropriate education.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Diabetes Technology.

question: How should sick-day rules be communicated to adults with diabetes?
gold_answer: Provide a clear plan for illness: continue hydration, monitor glucose (and ketones for those at risk), maintain carbohydrate intake, know when to seek medical care, and review medication adjustments with a clinician.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Sick-day management; local health service patient guides.

question: What foot-care self-management advice should be given?
gold_answer: Daily inspection, appropriate footwear, foot hygiene, prompt reporting of breaks in skin or infection signs, and avoidance of barefoot walking.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Foot Care; IWGDF patient education.

question: What are recommended screening intervals for peripheral neuropathy?
gold_answer: Assess for peripheral neuropathy at diagnosis of type 2 diabetes and at least annually thereafter using simple tests (e.g., monofilament).
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Neuropathy.

question: Should tobacco use be addressed at every visit for adults with diabetes?
gold_answer: Yes—assess and document tobacco use at every visit, advise cessation, and offer evidence-based support.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Tobacco and Substance Use.

question: What weight-management approach is supported for adults with type 2 diabetes and overweight or obesity?
gold_answer: Structured weight-management with nutrition, physical activity, behavioral strategies, and consideration of pharmacotherapy or metabolic surgery per eligibility and shared decision-making.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Obesity & Weight Management.

question: Is aspirin recommended for primary prevention in all adults with diabetes?
gold_answer: No—aspirin for primary prevention is not recommended for all; consider selectively in higher ASCVD risk with low bleeding risk after shared decision-making.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Cardiovascular Risk Management.

question: How often should eGFR be monitored in adults with diabetes and established CKD?
gold_answer: Monitor eGFR at least annually, and more frequently (e.g., every 3–6 months) as CKD progresses or after therapy changes that can affect kidney function.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, CKD & Risk Management; KDIGO monitoring.

question: Are culturally appropriate nutrition resources important in diabetes self-management education?
gold_answer: Yes—culturally tailored education and meal planning improve engagement and outcomes and should be incorporated when possible.
gold_source_ref: Diabetes self-management education standards; ADA Medical Nutrition Therapy.

question: Should adults with diabetes be screened for depression?
gold_answer: Yes—routine screening for depression and diabetes distress is recommended, with referral pathways for care.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Psychosocial Care.

question: Is carbohydrate counting required for all adults with type 2 diabetes?
gold_answer: Not required for everyone; select methods (carb counting, plate method, exchange lists) can be individualized to preferences and therapy.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Nutrition Therapy.

question: What advice is given about alcohol consumption for adults with diabetes?
gold_answer: If adults choose to drink alcohol, advise moderation and awareness of delayed hypoglycemia risk, especially when using insulin or certain medications.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Lifestyle Management.

question: When should referral to a diabetes educator or DSMES service be made?
gold_answer: At diagnosis, annually for ongoing support, when complicating factors occur, and when transitions in care happen.
gold_source_ref: Diabetes Self-Management Education and Support (DSMES) Consensus Report; ADA Standards of Care.

question: Is routine aspirin recommended for secondary prevention after ASCVD in adults with diabetes?
gold_answer: Yes—low-dose aspirin is generally recommended for secondary prevention in adults with diabetes and established ASCVD unless contraindicated.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Cardiovascular Disease & Risk Management.

question: Should adults with diabetes receive counseling on driving safety related to hypoglycemia risk?
gold_answer: Yes—discuss hypoglycemia recognition, checking glucose before driving if at risk, and not driving if hypoglycemic.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Hypoglycemia; driving safety statements.

question: Does reducing sedentary time have benefits beyond scheduled exercise for adults with diabetes?
gold_answer: Yes—breaking up prolonged sitting with light activity improves glycemic measures and is recommended in addition to planned exercise.
gold_source_ref: ADA Standards of Care, Physical Activity.

question: What is the exact starting insulin dose and titration schedule for a 72-kg adult with type 2 diabetes beginning basal insulin?
notes: Dosing/titration individualized; out of scope for this demo.

question: What is the recommended A1C target during pregnancy for women with pre-existing diabetes?
How should insulin be adjusted on the morning of elective surgery for a person with diabetes?
My 10-year-old child has type 1 diabetes—what basal insulin dose should we use?
What is the exact sliding-scale insulin regimen for managing high glucose during a viral illness at home?
Can you diagnose diabetes for me based on these symptoms I am experiencing right now?
notes: Direct diagnosis not provided; advise clinical evaluation.

question: What precise GLP-1 RA dose should I take to lose 10 kilograms in three months?
Is my chest pain right now a heart attack related to my diabetes?
What insulin-to-carbohydrate ratio should I use for dinner tonight?
